tools.name: get_human_state
tools.description:
Get current unified human state for a session. Call this before generating important responses.

    Returns:
    - state: calm | relaxed | focused | stressed | acute_stress
    - stress_score: 0-100 (lower = calmer)
    - confidence: 0.0-1.0 (based on signal quality and device type)
    - suggested

tools.name: ingest
tools.description:
Send biometric signals from any sensor, get unified state back.

    Required: session_id + timestamp (ISO 8601) + at least one signal.
    Send whatever you have — the API fuses all signals into one state.

    Common signals (highest impact):
    - heart_rate (bpm, 30-220) + rmssd (ms) — cardiovas

tools.name: get_trigger_memory
tools.description:
Retrieve psychological trigger profile for a subject.

    Returns which conversation topics consistently cause stress (active triggers) and which have been resolved over time.

    - active triggers: topics where stress was elevated across multiple sessions. Tread carefully.
    - resolved triggers

tools.name: get_session_history
tools.description:
Get state history for a session over time.

    Returns timestamped datapoints with stress_score, state, and heart_rate for each observation.
    Includes an overall trend: rising | falling | stable.

    Use minutes parameter to control the lookback window (default: 5, max: 60).
    Useful for dete

tools.name: request_api_key
tools.description:
Request a free Nefesh API key. No existing API key needed for this call.

    IMPORTANT: You MUST ask the user for their real email address before
    calling this tool. Do NOT invent, guess, or generate an email address.
    The user will receive a verification link they must click to activate

tools.name: check_api_key_status
tools.description:
Check the status of a pending API key request.

    Use the exact same email the user provided to request_api_key.
    Poll this every 10 seconds. Once the user clicks the verification
    link in their inbox, status changes from 'pending' to 'ready'
    and the response includes the API key. Store

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "request_api_key" references sensitive artifacts (api_key). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Request a free Nefesh API key. No existing API key needed for this call. IMPORTANT: You MUST ask the user for their real email address before calling this tool.…

evidence.snippet: Check the status of a pending API key request. Use the exact same email the user provided to request_api_key. Poll this every 10 seconds. Once the user clicks t…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: request_api_key, check_api_key_status · Private data: request_api_key, check_api_key_status · Public sink: ingest, request_api_key, check_api_key_status.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: request_api_key, check_api_key_status · Private data: request_api_key, check_api_key_status · Public sink: ingest, request_api_key, check_api_key_status

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
